title: Implement update_task MCP handler in fused-memory server (tools.py)
analysis: The update_task MCP handler is ALREADY FULLY IMPLEMENTED across all three layers: (1) tools.py line 760-791 — registered as @mcp.tool() with metadata dict→JSON coercion, (2) task_interceptor.py line 205-218 — middleware with event emission, (3) taskmaster_client.py line 207-224 — backend proxy. The -32601 'Unknown tool' error was caused by the systemd fused-memory service running stale code that predated the handler addition. The service has been restarted (active since ~28min ago) and both /health and tool registration verification confirm update_task is available. Existing tests cover: tool registration (test_health_endpoint.py:107), metadata coercion (dict/string/None), interceptor event emission (test_task_interceptor.py:217), and backend argument forwarding (test_taskmaster_client.py:100). The remaining work is hardening test coverage for MCP tool-level gaps: update_task with prompt parameter, append=True, and error handling when the interceptor raises.

steps.description: Add failing test: update_task with prompt parameter calls interceptor.update_task with correct arguments (prompt forwarded, append defaults False). Test in test_health_endpoint.py alongside existing metadata coercion tests.
Verify existing implementation passes the prompt test — no code change expected since tools.py already forwards prompt and append to interceptor.update_task.
Add failing test: update_task with append=True passes append=True through to interceptor.update_task.
Verify existing implementation passes the append test — no code change expected.
Add failing test: update_task error handling — when interceptor.update_task raises Exception, the MCP tool returns {'error': str(e)} instead of propagating.
Verify existing implementation passes the error handling test — tools.py already wraps in try/except returning {'error': str(e)}.

design_decisions.decision: No source code changes needed — handler already implemented on main
design_decisions.rationale: The update_task handler exists in all three layers (tools.py, task_interceptor.py, taskmaster_client.py) and is already deployed. The -32601 error was a stale systemd service running old code. The service has been restarted and verified healthy. Adding tests hardens against future regressions.

design_decisions.decision: Add tests to test_health_endpoint.py rather than creating a new test file
design_decisions.rationale: test_health_endpoint.py already contains update_task metadata coercion tests and has the mcp_server_with_tasks fixture that creates an MCP server with a mocked TaskInterceptor. Grouping all MCP tool-level update_task tests in one place follows the existing convention.

design_decisions.decision: Tests verify behavior through the MCP tool manager, not HTTP
design_decisions.rationale: Existing tests use mcp_server._tool_manager.call_tool() which tests the full tool function including parameter validation, metadata coercion, and interceptor delegation without needing HTTP transport. This is the established pattern in the codebase.

reuse.how: Reuse existing fixture that creates MCP server with mocked task_interceptor for testing tool-level behavior
Reuse AsyncMock-based interceptor fixture with update_task pre-mocked
Call tools through the MCP tool manager directly, matching the established test pattern for tool-level tests
